question: Compare the primary coolant chemistry control in NuScale with conventional PWR chemistry programs.

nuscale_ch05_0002.title: Summary Description
nuscale_ch05_0002.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping to transfer heat from the reactor core to the steam generator, ensuring safe and efficient operation. The RCS design supports up to twelve modules, maintains proper coolant chemistry and boron concentration, and is engineered to prevent fuel design limit violations during normal and off-normal conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: Each NuScale Power Module (NPM) contains two steam generators (SGs) integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The steam generator system (SGS) includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs. The steam generator tubes and associated welds are part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, ensuring safe separation between the primary and secondary sides.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes the main components of the NuScale reactor system, focusing on the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping, and the Pressurizer (PZR). The RPV serves as a primary barrier to fission product release and houses key reactor internals, while the RCS piping connects various system components and the PZR is integrated into the RPV to help control system pressure. Each component's functions and connections are briefly outlined, with references to further detailed sections.

nuscale_ch05_0010.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Materials
nuscale_ch05_0010.summary: The Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary (RCPB) materials, including welds, are selected and fabricated in accordance with ASME BPVC standards and meet the requirements of relevant U.S. regulations such as 10 CFR 50 and its appendices. These materials are designed, constructed, and tested to ensure high quality, environmental compatibility, and fracture resistance, with specific material specifications detailed in Table 5.2-4.

nuscale_ch05_0004.title: System Description
nuscale_ch05_0004.summary: The Reactor Coolant System (RCS) in the NuScale design is located inside the containment vessel and includes components such as the reactor pressure vessel, pressurizer, and associated piping and controls. It removes heat from the reactor core to the steam generators using natural circulation, and features systems for degasification and emergency core cooling to maintain safety and integrity of the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0003.title: Design Basis
nuscale_ch05_0003.summary: The Design Basis section outlines that the NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) uses natural circulation without pumps or external piping, ensuring safe and efficient heat transfer from the reactor core to the steam generator. The RCS is designed to maintain proper coolant flow, water chemistry, and boron concentration to protect fuel integrity and support safe operation under normal and transient conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the NuScale pressurizer and its components, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, heater sheaths, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0012.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Leakage Detection
nuscale_ch05_0012.summary: The NuScale reactor coolant system (RCS) does not use traditional components with designed leakage rates, making it impractical to distinguish between identified and unidentified leakage inside containment using current instrumentation. Any leakage into containment, regardless of source, quickly vaporizes and disperses, so all leakage is treated as unidentified until specifically located and quantified by other means; the leakage rate is determined by RCS inventory balance and compared to the containment evacuation system (CES) flow. The installed leakage detection systems are reliable, redundant, and sensitive enough to support leak-before-break analyses.

nuscale_ch05_0011.title: Reactor Coolant Pressure Boundary Inservice Inspection and Testing
nuscale_ch05_0011.summary: This section describes the materials and standards used for threaded fasteners in key reactor coolant system components, emphasizing the use of Alloy 718 for its compatibility and stress corrosion cracking resistance. It also outlines the requirements for preservice and inservice inspection and testing of pressure-retaining components, specifying adherence to the latest ASME BPVC Section XI codes and relevant regulatory conditions for ongoing inspection intervals.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It cites guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as specific ASME code cases relevant to reactor component materials, inspection, and safety requirements.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system, while Section B specifies the detailed components within that system—including the steam generators—which are relevant to understanding how primary coolant chemistry is controlled and maintained.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system, while Section B specifies operational details and chemistry control actions (such as water level adjustments and degasification) that are implemented within the system described in Section A.

Section A describes the overall design and flow characteristics of the NuScale reactor coolant system, while Section B specifies the material standards and regulatory compliance for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, both of which are foundational to establishing and maintaining appropriate primary coolant chemistry control.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including steam generators and associated subsystems, while Section B details operational procedures and chemistry control measures (such as wet layup and chemical volume control) for these components, showing how the system's design enables specific coolant chemistry management practices.

Section A describes the components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including steam generators that are directly affected by coolant chemistry, while Section B specifies the material standards and regulatory requirements for the reactor coolant pressure boundary, which are influenced by the effectiveness of coolant chemistry control in preventing corrosion and degradation.

Section A describes the operational procedures and chemistry control systems (such as the chemical volume control system) used to manage coolant chemistry, which directly affect the integrity and performance of the reactor coolant pressure boundary materials discussed in Section B.

Section A details specific features of the NuScale RCS related to coolant chemistry control (degasification and gas removal), while Section B provides a general overview of the RCS components and their functions, with Section A specifying operational details for the general system described in Section B.

Section A describes the RCS degasification line and vent valves for removing noncondensible gases, while Section B lists the steam generators and associated RCS components, including the degasification vent lines, showing that both sections detail interconnected RCS subsystems relevant to coolant chemistry control.

Section A describes the function of the degasification line and reactor vent valves in removing noncondensible gases from the pressurizer during both normal and emergency operations, while Section B provides specific operational steps and component actions (such as venting nitrogen and using the chemical volume control system) that implement and detail the chemistry control processes outlined in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system design, while Section B specifies the detailed components within that system, including the steam generators, thus elaborating on the general description in Section A.

Section A provides a general overview of the NuScale reactor coolant system design, while Section B specifies operational procedures and system components (such as the chemical volume control system) that implement and control the coolant chemistry described in Section A.

Section A describes specific features (degasification line and vent valves) for removing noncondensible gases from the primary coolant, which are part of the overall RCS described in Section B as providing circulation and containment of the primary coolant.

Section A provides detailed material specifications for pressurizer components that are generally described as part of the reactor coolant system in Section B, connecting specific design details to the overall system summary.

Section A details the materials and components of the pressurizer, while Section B provides an overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the pressurizer and steam generators, thus Section A specifies details for the general system described in Section B.

Section A describes the materials and components of the NuScale pressurizer, while Section B outlines operational procedures involving the pressurizer and associated systems, with both sections connected through their focus on the pressurizer's role in coolant chemistry control and system operation.

Section B explains that the NuScale RCS lacks traditional components like pumps and external piping, which Section A references as absent when discussing leakage detection challenges, connecting the system design to its operational implications.

Section A discusses the challenges in detecting and distinguishing reactor coolant pressure boundary leakage in NuScale's integrated design, while Section B describes the integrated configuration of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, which is the underlying design feature affecting leakage detection methods.

Section A discusses the challenges in detecting and distinguishing reactor coolant system (RCS) leakage in NuScale due to unique system design, while Section B describes the use of the chemical volume control system and other components to manage coolant chemistry and system conditions, both addressing aspects of primary coolant chemistry control in NuScale as compared to conventional PWRs.

Section A specifies the material selection and compatibility of threaded fasteners with the borated water environment in the RCS, which supports the overall RCS design described in Section B by ensuring material integrity under the primary coolant chemistry conditions.

Section A describes the selection of threaded fastener materials for compatibility with the borated water environment in the RCS, while Section B outlines the components contained within the reactor pressure vessel, including those affected by coolant chemistry, thus Section A provides specific material compatibility details relevant to the general system described in Section B.

Section A discusses the selection of threaded fastener materials for compatibility with the borated water environment in the reactor coolant system, while Section B describes operational procedures that involve controlling water chemistry (such as wet layup and degasification) in the primary system, both addressing aspects of primary coolant chemistry control in NuScale.

Section A describes the materials and components of the NuScale pressurizer, while Section B discusses the detection of leakage from the reactor coolant pressure boundary, both of which are connected through their relevance to maintaining the integrity and chemistry control of the primary coolant system.

Section A describes the materials used in the pressurizer components, while Section B specifies the material selection and compatibility requirements for threaded fasteners in the reactor coolant pressure boundary, both of which are connected through their focus on material compatibility with the borated water environment in the primary coolant system.

Section B specifies the material selection and compatibility of threaded fasteners with reactor coolant chemistry, which supports the leakage detection considerations discussed in Section A by ensuring fastener integrity in the borated water environment.

Section A cites industry guidelines and standards relevant to primary coolant chemistry control, which provide the regulatory and technical basis for the system described in Section B's summary of the NuScale reactor coolant system design.

Section A references industry guidelines and standards for primary water chemistry control, which provide the regulatory and technical basis for the design and operation of reactor coolant system components described in Section B, including the steam generators.

Section A lists references, including EPRI PWR Primary Water Chemistry Guidelines, which provide the regulatory and industry standards that inform the chemistry control practices described in Section B for NuScale's system components.
